reasoning: First step: ingest all patient PDFs

demographics.text:
ADMISSION NOTE
==============
Patient Name: Margaret Chen
MRN: MRN-884721
Date of Birth: 03/14/1958
Age: 66 years old
Gender: Female

Admission Date: 05/12/2024

ADMITTING DIAGNOSIS: Diabetic Ketoacidosis (DKA)

ALLERGIES: Penicillin (hives)

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Patient presents with diabetic ketoacidosis (dka).

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
66F with known T2DM presenting with DKA. pH 7.21 on admission. Started on insulin drip; transitioned to subcutaneous insulin after anion gap closure. Received 3L IV NS. Potassium repleted. Blood glucose normalized. Nephrology consulted for CKD monitoring.

ADMISSION MEDICATIONS:
- Metformin 1000 mg BID
- Lisinopril 10 mg Daily
- Atorvastatin 40 mg Nightly
- Aspirin 81 mg Daily


VITAL SIGNS ON ADMISSION:
Blood Pressure: 138/88 mmHg
Heart Rate: 96 bpm
Temperature: 37.8 C
Respiratory Rate: 18/min
O2 Saturation: 97% on room air

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
General: Alert and oriented x3. Mildly distressed.
Pertinent findings per admitting note.

PLAN:
- IV access x2
- Continuous cardiac monitoring
- Urinalysis
- Chest X-ray (unremarkable)

Signed: Dr. [Admitting Physician]

PROGRESS NOTE � Day 2
======================
Patient: Margaret Chen    MRN: MRN-884721
Date: 05/13/2024

SUBJECTIVE:
Patient reports improved symptoms. Tolerating medications.

OBJECTIVE:
Vitals: BP 132/80, HR 88, Temp 37.2, RR 16, O2 Sat 98% RA

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
Discharge Diagnosis: Urinary Tract Infection

#1. Hypertension
#2. Chronic Kidney Disease Stage 3
#3. Obesity

Plan:
- Continue current medications
- Monitor labs
- Urine culture (sent 05/16) � result pending
- HbA1c � pending (sent to reference lab)


Signed: Dr. [Covering Physician]

DISCHARGE SUMMARY
=================
Patient Name: Margaret Chen
MRN: MRN-884721
Date of Birth: 03/14/1958
Admission Date: 05/12/2024
Discharge Date: 05/17/2024

PRINCIPAL DIAGNOSIS: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus with Diabetic Ketoacidosis

SECONDARY DIAGNOSES:
- Hypertension
- Chronic Kidney Disease Stage 3
- Obesity


DISCHARGE CONDITION: Stable

ALLERGIES: Penicillin (hives)

HOSPITAL COURSE:
66F with known T2DM presenting with DKA. pH 7.21 on admission. Started on insulin drip; transitioned to subcutaneous insulin after anion gap closure. Received 3L IV NS. Potassium repleted. Blood glucose normalized. Nephrology consulted for CKD monitoring.

PROCEDURES PERFORMED:
- IV access x2
- Continuous cardiac monitoring
- Urinalysis
- Chest X-ray (unremarkable)


DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS:
- Insulin Glargine 20 units SC Nightly
- Lisinopril 20 mg PO Daily
- Atorvastatin 40 mg PO Nightly
- Aspirin 81 mg PO Daily


PENDING RESULTS AT DISCHARGE:
- Urine culture (sent 05/16) � result pending (PENDING)
- HbA1c � pending (sent to reference lab) (PENDING)


FOLLOW-UP INSTRUCTIONS:
- Follow up with primary care physician in 1 week
- Endocrinology referral � appointment to be scheduled
- Recheck BMP in 1 week


Signed: Dr. [Discharging Physician]

LABORATORY RESULTS
==================
Patient: Margaret Chen    MRN: MRN-884721

COMPLETE BLOOD COUNT (Admission):
WBC:        11.2  [4.5-11.0]  H
Hemoglobin: 12.8  [12.0-16.0]
Hematocrit: 38.5  [36.0-48.0]
Platelets:  245   [150-400]

COMPREHENSIVE METABOLIC PANEL:
Sodium:     138   [136-145]
Potassium:  3.2   [3.5-5.1]   L
Chloride:   101   [98-107]
CO2:        18    [22-29]     L
BUN:        24    [7-20]      H
Creatinine: 1.4   [0.6-1.2]   H
Glucose:    412   [70-100]    H
eGFR:       48    mL/min      (CKD Stage 3)

PENDING RESULTS:
Urine culture (sent 05/16) � result pending
HbA1c � pending (sent to reference lab)


Report generated by: Laboratory Information System

MEDICATION ADMINISTRATION RECORD
==================================
Patient: Margaret Chen    MRN: MRN-884721
Period: 05/12/2024 to 05/17/2024

ADMISSION MEDICATIONS (Home Medications on Admission):
Metformin  1000 mg  PO  BID
Lisinopril  10 mg  PO  Daily
Atorvastatin  40 mg  PO  Nightly
Aspirin  81 mg  PO  Daily


DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS:
Insulin Glargine  20 units  SC  Nightly
Lisinopril  20 mg  PO  Daily
Atorvastatin  40 mg  PO  Nightly
Aspirin  81 mg  PO  Daily


NOTE: Changes from admission medications require clinician reconciliation.

66F with known T2DM presenting with DKA. pH 7.21 on admission. Started on insulin drip; transitioned to subcutaneous insulin after anion gap closure. Received 3L IV NS. Potassium repleted. Blood glucose normalized. Nephrology consulted for CKD monitoring.

PROCEDURES PERFORMED:
- IV access x2
- Continuous cardiac monitoring
- Urinalysis
- Chest X-ray (unremarkable)


DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS:
- Insulin Glargine 20 units SC Nightly
- Lisinopril 20 mg PO Daily
- Atorvastatin 40 mg PO Nightly
- Aspirin 81 mg PO Daily


PENDING RESULTS AT DISCHARGE:
- Urine culture (sent 05/16) � result pending (PENDING)
- HbA1c � pending (sent to reference lab) (PENDING)


FOLLOW-UP INSTRUCTIONS:
- Follow up with primary care physician in 1 week
- Endocrinology referral � appointment to be scheduled
- Recheck BMP in 1 week


Signed: Dr. [Discharging Physician]
